headline: Sepsis Survivor Becomes High School Track Star
meta_description: A teen who lost his arms and legs to sepsis at age 3 is now a competitive high school track athlete aiming for the Paralympics.
summary_short: Despite losing both arms and legs to sepsis in childhood, a teenager has become a high school track star with Paralympic ambitions.
body:
At three years old, a serious sepsis infection threatened to claim more than this child's life—it claimed his limbs. After losing both arms and legs, he faced a future that many would consider impossibly limited. Yet through determination and modern adaptive athletics, he has written a different story.

Now a high school athlete, he competes on the track team, proving that physical limitation need not define one's capabilities. His participation in competitive sports alongside his able-bodied peers demonstrates the power of accessibility and inclusion in youth athletics.

With his sights set on the Paralympics, he represents a broader shift in how society views disability and athletic achievement. His journey shows that resilience and opportunity can transform tragedy into inspiration, and that young people facing extraordinary challenges can still pursue dreams that once seemed impossible.
